evals.prompt: help me write up this incident: operations/incident-summary/fixtures/log_grounded_incident.log
evals.expected_output: The skill runs the shared triage engine to ground a timeline skeleton from real timestamps, then interviews the user one question at a time about true start time, blast radius, trigger vs. root cause, resolution (fix vs. mitigation), and action items, finally writing INCIDENT_SUMMARY.md with a SEV tier, timeline, impact, trigger, root cause, resolution, and tagged action items.
assertions: The timeline is grounded in real timestamps extracted from the log file where available
Questions are asked one at a time, not as a single bulk form
The report explicitly tags the resolution as either a fix or a mitigation
Action items are each tagged as preventing recurrence or improving detection/response

evals.prompt: help me write up this incident, I only have some notes: operations/incident-summary/fixtures/notes_only_scenario.txt
evals.expected_output: With no log file given, the skill works entirely from the human notes, extracting any concrete facts already stated before interviewing for the rest, and still produces a full INCIDENT_SUMMARY.md with an approximate (not fabricated) timeline where exact times aren't known.
assertions: The skill does not require or ask for a log file
Timestamps not grounded in a real source are marked approximate, not stated as exact
SEV tier is asked directly rather than inferred from anything
